key_insight: A stacked machine-learning and neural-network approach can help detect early Alzheimer’s disease from clinical, cognitive, and neuroimaging-derived data while highlighting potentially important biomarkers.
clinical_relevance: Earlier identification of likely early-stage Alzheimer’s disease could support timelier evaluation and care planning, though it is not a standalone diagnostic tool.
method: Used ADNI data with iterative imputation to handle missing values and Borderline SVM-SMOTE to address class imbalance.
Performed wrapper-based and embedded feature selection to keep only the most informative variables.
Trained a stacking ensemble (Logistic Regression, Extra Trees, Bagging KNN, LightGBM) and an artificial neural network, then compared performance.
evidence_signal: Model performance was assessed with precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC on selected features split into training and testing sets.
watch_item: The approach depends on retrospective ADNI data and careful external validation is needed before generalizing to real-world clinical settings.
paper_title: Predicting Early Stages Of Alzheimer's Disease And Identifying Key Biomarkers Using Deep Artificial Neural Network And Ensemble Of Machine Learning Methodologies
